attacking_style: Attacks just before categorized climbs (50-60km to go) from large breakaways, showing patience to wait for optimal gradient. In sprints, opens at 200m marker with precise timing, not raw power.
team_tactics: Heavily reliant on structured team support: Quick-Step delivered perfect positioning and full-day control (2017); severely exposed when isolated (CCC 2020). Uses teammates in breakaways for pacing before final sprint.
terrain_preference: Reduced bunch sprints after crashes/attrition and punchy finales with short climbs. Excels parasiting other teams' leadouts when own support limited. Struggles in pure flat power sprints and sustained classics efforts.
vulnerability: Top-end sprint speed deficit against pure sprinters (beaten by Jacobsen despite perfect position). Cannot sustain bridge efforts in classics. Collapses tactically when team absent or mis-paces efforts.
key_patterns: Contested intermediate sprints and categorized climbs for points across multiple stage races (2017, 2020, 2023)
Won four sprints from reduced bunches after crashes eliminated leadout trains (2018 x2, 2017 x2)
Positioned perfectly (top-3 wheels) but lacked acceleration to convert against faster finishers (2021 Vuelta x2)
Quick-Step provided full-day control and perfect positioning; CCC/UAE stints showed tactical isolation and abandons
Opened sprints at precise 200m marker showing tactical discipline over raw power (2018 x2)
Attacked successfully from large breakaways before climbs with 50-60km remaining (2019 Tour stage win)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed aggression in points competition but inability to finish classics work suggests declining sustained power while tactical acumen remains
